title: notifications core — channel enum reference
description: The canonical list of channel slugs used across the notifications wave. NOT a seeded table — channels are compile-time enum values (referenced by NotificationDelivery.channel, NotificationTemplate.channel, NotificationPreference.channel, NotificationCategory.default_channels, NotificationDigest.channel). This file is the single source of truth; each channel module registers its NotificationChannelInterface implementation into `NotificationChannelRegistry` at boot referencing its slug here.

channels.description: Persisted in the Notification table with channel=in_app + broadcast to `user.{id}.notifications` via Reverb for live inbox updates. No external provider dependency.
Wraps Laravel Mail (MailManager) with MJML → HTML pre-rendering via React Email at CI build time. Providers: Mailgun, SendGrid, AWS SES.
channels.cost_per_send_kind: per-provider (typically free for volume, priced at higher tiers)

channels.description: Mobile + web push. Own MultipleInstanceManager wrapping FCM (Android + Web), APNs (iOS), Expo (React Native), OneSignal (enterprise). Requires a registered PushSubscription device token.
Own MultipleInstanceManager wrapping Twilio, MessageBird, Vonage, Plivo, AWS SNS. Cost per message tracked in NotificationDelivery.cost_micro_units. STOP-keyword handling in the SMS module.

notes: `default_priority_order` is only a HINT to the resolver when multiple channels are equally preferred — it defaults to in-app first (cheapest), mail second (broadest reach), push third, sms last (most expensive).

Channels can be added by publishing a new channel module + adding an entry here + rebuilding. Feature-flag `notifications.<channel>` gates the channel until GA. No hard-coded enum in PHP — CI generates the PHP enum from this file at build time.
For future channels (slack, teams, webhook, etc.), extend this file and ship a matching channel module.
